details: @keeneye/toolkit@5.0.1 is a malicious second-stage dropper providing no real functionality (dist/index.js re-exports a src/index.js absent from the tarball). Its heavily obfuscated postinstall (560+ _0x identifiers) downloads a script from a remote keeneye-controlled host (config[.]keeneye[.]io) and executes it via spawn(node,[downloaded_file]); the README misrepresents this as "telemetry". It shares an identical structure with @keeneye/uikit (same namespace, same version 5.0.1, same obfuscated postinstall dropper). @keeneye/toolkit@5.0.1 is an install-time RCE dropper. The postinstall.js (13.5KB, 562 obfuscated identifiers) downloads platform-specific payloads via HTTP/HTTPS and spawns them as child processes. The package's dist/index.js requires a non-existent src/index.js — zero runtime functionality, purely a delivery vehicle. Version history is fabricated (first publish claims 5.0.1). The README admits 'telemetry' to telemetry[.]keeneye[.]io as a cover story, but the code downloads and executes arbitrary binaries, not telemetry posts. No direct token-theft markers found.js' (JS-level, not shell), bypassing shell-level detectors. This is a classic remote code execution supply-chain attack pattern.
